steps.n: Changes from a root meaning 'to call' into a question word meaning 'which/what'
Adding vocative particle يٰ to call attention
Adding a small mark (damma) to call out to someone, like saying 'hey you!'
Adding ها makes it more formal when calling someone
Added shadda to show intensive wrapping action
Added مُ at beginning and changed vowels to make it describe who does the action

steps.m: the one who wraps himself (a title for Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show this is being used as a title/name. This is referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ who used to wrap himself in his cloak.
Changed root letters into a command by adding 'oo' sound and quiet ending
Weak letter و removed and kasra added at end to make emphatic command. This is used in the ayah as a strong command from Allah to the Prophet ﷺ to pray at night.
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make it a noun
Added 'al' (the) at the beginning and a fatHa mark at the end because it's receiving the action of 'stand'
This is a basic exception particle that doesn't come from a root. It's used to exclude something from what was mentioned before.

steps.m: except (what comes after it from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding shadda and alif makes the exception stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it's excluding قَلِيلًا (a little) from ٱلَّيْلَ (the night) - meaning pray all night except a little of it.
The ي makes it into a word describing something that is small in amount

steps.m: a little (describing how much of قُمِ ٱلَّيْلَ)
steps.n: Added ًا (tanween) at the end to make it an adverb - tells us how much of something
Changed the verb into a noun by adding vowel marks. Now it means the result (half) instead of the action (splitting).
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is the object of something
Added 'hu' at the end to show possession - 'its half'
Small waw helps show correct pronunciation of hu
This is the basic form of the word that means 'or' - it connects two choices
Added vowel marks to help with pronunciation - fatha (َ) on أ and kasra (ِ) on و because it's followed by a word starting with a connecting hamza
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added alif and sukun marks to make it a command form
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'

steps.m: from it/him (referring to the night mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (it/him) to show what we're taking from
Root letters connected with vowels to make a descriptive word meaning 'little/few'
Added ي to make it an adjective that describes how something is little/small
Added -an ending to show it's being used as a direct object (something is being decreased by a little amount)
This is a basic connecting word that gives a choice between options. In this ayah, it connects to the word زِدْ (add/increase), giving the meaning 'or increase'
Added kasra to show it's a command form base
The weak letter ي is dropped and replaced with sukun because it's a command form. This is a common pattern with hollow verbs (verbs with ي or و in the middle).
This is a preposition showing location or direction
The ى changes to يْ to connect with the next part. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon him/it (referring to what was mentioned in أَوْ زِدْ)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this is happening to 'it/him'. This connects back to what was mentioned in the previous phrase أَوْ زِدْ (or increase).
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show doing something with extra care and attention
Changed to a command form by adding sukun at the end

steps.m: and recite carefully! (command to recite الْقُرْءَانَ)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it to previous commands. Changed the ending to connect it to the next word (the Quran)

steps.m: to read, recite, or gather together

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: recitation, that which is recited abundantly
steps.n: Changes verb 'read' into noun meaning 'that which is read/recited' with emphasis on abundance
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and َ to show it's receiving the action of the verb رَتِّلِ (recite) in this ayah

steps.m: to arrange something carefully and beautifully

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled middle letter (ت) to make it mean 'he recited slowly and clearly'
Changed from a verb (action) to a noun (thing/concept)

steps.m: careful and beautiful recitation (describing how to recite القُرْءَانَ)
steps.n: Added tanwin to show this describes how the action should be done
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like saying 'Listen!' before important news

steps.m: indeed we (subject of سَنُلْقِى - we will cast)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. This connects to the next word سَنُلْقِى (we will cast) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'we' as subject

steps.m: we will throw (onto عَلَيْكَ - upon you)
steps.n: Added future tense marker س to indicate 'will'
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'. This is a particle word that doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: upon you (receiving قَوْلًا ثَقِيلًا - heavy word)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this affects 'you'. The ي is added before ك to connect them smoothly. This word shows who will receive the 'heavy word' mentioned later in the ayah.
Basic meaning of speaking or saying
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a noun. This word is what will be sent down (object of سَنُلْقِى - We will cast down).

steps.m: a weighty speech (what will be sent down)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is indefinite. This speech is described as ثَقِيلًا (weighty/heavy) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changes verb into an adjective describing the word/speech mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: heavy (describing قَوْلًا - word/speech that is being sent down)
steps.n: Adds tanween alif to show this adjective is describing the word/speech that is being sent down

Adding the shadda (double letter mark) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes how special the night prayer (نَاشِئَةَ) is.
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone/something that does the action
Adding ة turns the 'one who rises' into 'the act of rising' (specifically at night)
The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence. It's connected to 'the night' (ٱلَّيْلِ) which comes right after it.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added the definite article ال which becomes assimilated with the ل, shown by the shadda
Added the kasra ending to show this word is possessed by نَاشِئَةَ (rising/hours) in the phrase نَاشِئَةَ ٱلَّيْلِ (rising of the night/night hours)
Basic form of the feminine pronoun that refers back to something mentioned before
Added kasra (ِ) under ه to show this is an independent pronoun, not attached to another word
Added fatha (َ) after ى to help with pronunciation when continuing to read
This root carries the basic meaning of strength or intensity
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to make it an adjective describing the quality of being strong
Added أَ at the beginning and changed the word pattern to make it comparative. This word is being compared to وَطْـًٔا (impact) in this ayah.
Changed the verb into a noun showing the action itself

steps.m: in stepping/impact (specifying what is most intense in أَشَدُّ)
steps.n: This word explains what exactly is 'most intense' (أَشَدُّ) during the night - it's the impact or effect of worship
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'straight' to 'straighter' by adding أَ at start - compares with وَطْـًٔا to show it's more intense/stronger
Adds coordinating conjunction to connect with previous phrase
Connected the root letters together to make a noun for the act of speaking
Changed the vowel to kasra to make a different form of the noun
Changed to passive by adding ي between first and second root letters

steps.m: a saying (as something being acted upon)
steps.n: Added -an ending to turn 'it was said' into 'a saying' (like changing 'spoke' to 'speech')

steps.m: indeed (emphasizes لَكَ 'for you' which comes after)
steps.n: This is a special word that adds emphasis. It's like adding an exclamation mark in English. In this ayah, it makes the next word لَكَ (for you) stronger and more certain.
Basic preposition indicating direction or purpose

steps.m: for you (speaking to one male) (connected to سَبْحًا)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ك to show who the action is for. This connects to the word سَبْحًا (occupation/activity) in the ayah, showing you have this activity.
Basic preposition showing location or time. In this ayah, it connects to 'the day' to show when something happens.
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The addition of alif creates a noun meaning the time when light flows/spreads
Adding definite article with shadda on following letter

steps.m: of the daytime (in فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ - in the daytime)
steps.n: Added -i ending because it comes after the word 'in' (في)
Basic meaning of moving freely through space
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed from a past tense verb (he did it) to just naming the action itself by making the middle letter silent

steps.m: moving freely/swimming/gliding (object of لَكَ - for you)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif to show this is the object being given to 'you' in the phrase
Basic meaning of length or extension
Added vowels and ي to make it describe a quality of length

steps.m: for a long time (describing سَبْحًا/floating free)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the floating free (سَبْحًا) happens - for a long time during the day
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he remembered' to a command by adding 'u' at start and putting sukoon and dammah marks - like saying 'you remember!'
Added 'and' at beginning and changed ending vowel to connect with the next word اسْمَ (name)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Forms the noun 'name' from the concept of being elevated. This word is what is being remembered/mentioned in the command اذْكُرِ (remember/mention).

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show this is someone who does this caring perfectly
Added kasra to show this lord belongs to something (in this case, it will belong to 'you')
Added كَ at the end to show this is your Lord. This whole word is possessed by اسم (name) from earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added ت at start and doubled middle letter to show doing action to oneself intensely
Changed from 'he devoted' to 'you devote!' by adding a stop mark (sukun) at the end to make it a command
Added و at beginning to connect with previous command
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
The ى changes to ي before adding the pronoun
Added the pronoun هِ meaning 'him' to show who is being directed to
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed from verb to noun showing intensity of devotion
Added -an ending to show how the action (devoting) was done, connecting it to the verb before it

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and have authority over

steps.n: Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete mastery/lordship

steps.m: The Lord (owner of المشرق والمغرب - the East and West)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the subject/doer, and the ّ (shadda) makes the 'r' sound stronger to emphasize 'The Lord'

steps.m: to shine, to rise (like the sun)

steps.n: Added م at the beginning to show where something happens, like how مسجد (mosque) is where سجد (prostration) happens

steps.m: the east (connected to رَّبُّ - Lord of)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific - not just any east, but THE east. This word is connected to رَّبُّ (Lord) showing that Allah is Lord of the east

steps.m: of the east (possessed by رَّبُّ - Lord)
steps.n: The ِ ending shows this belongs to or is possessed by رَّبُّ (Lord), like saying "Lord OF the east"

steps.m: to set (like the sun), to go westward

steps.n: Adding مَ at the start and ِ in the middle makes this mean 'the place where something happens' - in this case, where the sun sets

steps.m: and the place of sunset (connected to المَشْرِقِ)
steps.n: Adding و connects this word to المَشْرِقِ (the east) mentioned just before it in the ayah
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific direction of west, paired with east (المَشْرِقِ) in the ayah
The kasra ending shows this belongs to رَّبُّ (Lord) mentioned at the start of the ayah, making it mean 'Lord of the west'
Basic negation particle that means 'no' or 'not'
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation

The alif is elongated (آ) to add emphasis to the negation. This is especially important here because it's part of the phrase 'لَآ إِلٰهَ' meaning 'there is no god' which is a strong statement of monotheism.

steps.m: to be devoted to, to worship

steps.n: Changes from verb (to worship) into noun (object of worship)
The small alif above makes the 'aa' sound longer when reading
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of لَآ (no/there is no)
This is the base form of the exception particle that comes after negation to mean 'except' or 'but'. In this ayah, it follows لَآ (no) to create the meaning 'there is no (deity)... except'
Added shadda and alif to complete the word. The shadda emphasizes the 'l' sound, and the alif extends the final vowel.

steps.m: He (referring to Allah mentioned as رَبُّ/Lord)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to Allah who was mentioned as رَبُّ (Lord) earlier in the ayah. It's used to emphasize that He alone is the Lord of the east and west.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِتَّ to show doing something for oneself. The ت combines with خ to make تّ
Changed from 'he took' to 'you take!' by changing the middle vowel and ending - makes it a command to one person
Added هُ to show who is being taken
Added فَ (so/then) at start. The اِ changes to ٱ when connected to فَ. This word connects to وَكِيلًا (as a guardian/trustee) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it into a past tense verb

steps.m: guardian, trustee, one who is entrusted
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters with voweling pattern to create a noun meaning someone who is trusted to handle affairs

steps.m: as a guardian/trustee (object of فَٱتَّخِذْهُ - take him)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of the verb فَٱتَّخِذْهُ (take him). The full meaning is 'take Him as a guardian'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he was patient' to 'be patient!' by adding alif at start and changing vowels to make it a command

steps.m: and be patient! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ regarding what they say)
steps.n: The و connects this command to previous instructions, while showing patience towards what the disbelievers say
This is the basic form of the word, related to the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition that shows direction or connection. Here it connects to what 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ), showing what to be patient about.
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the long 'aa' sound at the end
This is a connecting word that links 'be patient upon' with 'they say'. It means 'what' or 'that which' and helps join these ideas together - 'be patient upon what they say'
Added vowels to make the basic past tense. The middle و changes to ا which is common for this type of word.
Added يَ at start to show it's happening now. The middle vowels change because it's present tense.
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he left' to 'you leave!' by adding ٱ at the start - makes it a command to one person
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being abandoned (them)

steps.m: and abandon them! (command to Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this command with the previous command وَٱصْبِرْ (and be patient)
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that means the action itself

steps.m: a graceful abandonment (describing how to abandon them in وَٱهْجُرْهُمْ)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this describes how the abandonment should be done. This connects to the command 'abandon them' (وَٱهْجُرْهُمْ) earlier in the verse, explaining that it should be done gracefully.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to form an adjective describing the quality of beauty

steps.m: beautifully (describes how هَجْرًا/departure should be done)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is describing how the departure (هَجْرًا) should be done - in a beautiful/graceful way

steps.m: to leave something or someone alone

steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of leaving'
Adding the vowel mark to complete the command form

steps.m: and leave me! (command to one person)
steps.n: Added نِى at the end to show the command is directed at 'me'

steps.m: to deny, to reject as false

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows doing the action strongly or repeatedly
Changed from a past-tense verb (denied/rejected) to a noun describing someone who does this action repeatedly
The ين ending makes it plural (more than two) and shows it comes after a word that affects its ending
Added the definite article ال to make the word definite ('the deniers' instead of just 'deniers')

steps.m: and the ones who intensely deny/reject (connected to وَذَرْنِى)
steps.n: Added و to connect this word to the previous phrase وَذَرْنِى (and leave me with)
This is a special word that always comes before another noun to show possession or association. In this ayah, it comes before النَّعْمَةِ (luxury/comfort) to mean 'those of luxury'.
Added ى at the end to show this word is being used to describe or connect to something else. Here it connects to النَّعْمَةِ (luxury/comfort).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to feminine noun indicating single instance or state of comfort/blessing
Added definite article and genitive case ending to show possession/belonging
Changed to command form by adding shadda, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding sukun at end
Added 'them' at the end to show who should be given time (referring to المكذبين - the deniers mentioned in the previous ayah)

steps.m: and give them time! (command, connected to وَذَرْنِى - and leave me)
steps.n: Added 'and' to connect this command with the previous command 'leave me' (وَذَرْنِى)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to make an adjective describing the quality

steps.m: a little while (describes وَمَهِّلْهُمْ - give them respite)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to make it describe how long to give respite - connects to the command وَمَهِّلْهُمْ (give them respite)
This is the basic form of the particle that shows conditions or possibilities

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizes لَدَيْنَآ أَنكَالًا وَجَحِيمًا - 'with Us are shackles and burning fire')
steps.n: Adding the shadda changes this from a conditional particle to an emphasis particle. It makes what comes after it stronger and more certain.
This is a special word that shows location or possession. It has no root because it's a basic word in Arabic.
Added نا at the end to show this is happening with/to 'us'. In this ayah, it's connected to the word أَنكَالًا (shackles) to show the shackles are with us.
Changed to a noun pattern showing the physical thing used for restraining
Changed to plural form أَفْعَال which is commonly used for tools and instruments
Added ًا to show this word is the object of لَدَيْنَآ (we have), indicating these are the restraints that are possessed
Adding ي makes it mean 'extremely/intensely' hot
Added و to connect this word with أَنكَالًا (shackles) from the previous part of the verse

steps.m: and hellfire (object of إِنَّ لَدَيْنَآ - indeed, with Us)
steps.n: Added an -an ending (tanween) to show this is a direct object - one of the things that Allah has
The root letters ط-ع-م (about feeding) change to make طَعَام which means the actual food itself. Like how 'to feed' becomes 'food'. The word connects to what Allah prepared, mentioned before.
Adds tanween fatHa (ًا) to show this is one of the things being mentioned in the list of what Allah has prepared

steps.m: and food (coordinated with previous items in verse 73:12)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect it with the previous items Allah has prepared mentioned in verse 73:12
This is a special word that here means 'having' or 'possessing'. It connects the word before it (food) with the word after it (choking) to mean 'food that has choking'
Added fatha marks to show correct pronunciation
Added damma and shadda to create the base noun form

steps.m: a choking thing (food that gets stuck)
steps.n: Adding -ah at the end turns 'choking' into 'a thing that chokes/gets stuck'
Added kasratain to show this describes the food mentioned earlier in the verse
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun describing the result of the action
The ًا at the end shows this is one of the things being listed along with وَطَعَامًا (food)
Adds conjunction 'and' to connect this word to previous items in the list
Letters أ-ل-م got short vowels (َ ِ) to make the adjective 'painful'
Added ي to show this is an intense or lasting quality
Added ًا to show this word is describing the punishment (عَذَابًا) mentioned in the ayah
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case

steps.m: to shake, quake, or tremble violently

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'it shook'
Changed to present tense verb for feminine subject (like 'she' or 'it')

steps.m: earth, land, ground (as a basic concept)

steps.n: Basic root letters get vowels added to make the common noun meaning 'earth'. Like turning 'e-r-th' into 'earth'.
Adds 'the' to make it specific and ُ to show it's doing the shaking in يَوْمَ تَرْجُفُ ٱلْأَرْضُ (the day when the earth shakes)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changed the vowel pattern to make it plural, showing many mountains
Added ال to specify these particular mountains
Added و to connect this with what came before it in the verse

steps.m: and the mountains (subject of تَرْجُفُ - shake/quake)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the shaking/quaking mentioned in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This word tells us about what happened to الْجِبَالُ (the mountains) in the past.

steps.m: it was (referring to الْجِبَالُ - the mountains)
steps.n: Added ت with kasra at the end to show we're talking about a feminine word (الْجِبَالُ/mountains is feminine in Arabic)

steps.m: and it was (connecting to what happened before)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this description with the previous phrase about the earth shaking
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changed vowel pattern to show many mountains
Added ٱلْ to make it specific mountains, and ُ to show it's doing the shaking/trembling action with the earth in this verse

steps.m: a sand dune, heap of sand
steps.n: Changes from basic root meaning to a noun describing a pile/heap
Added tanwin alif to show this is the object describing what the mountains became. This word follows كانت (was/were) in the ayah.
Changes to describe something that is being scattered or poured
Added مَ at the beginning to show this is something that receives the action
Added emphasis to show completeness of the action
Added -an ending to show this is describing how the mountains crumbled and poured out like sand
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain
The long alif at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. This word connects to أَرْسَلْنَآ (we sent) in this ayah, showing who did the sending.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he caused to be sent/he sent forth
steps.n: Adding أ (hamza) at the start changes 'sent' to 'caused to send' - like turning 'send' into 'make someone send'

steps.m: we sent forth (connected to إِنَّآ - indeed we)
steps.n: Adding نَا at the end makes it 'we' instead of 'he'. The long alif (آ) at the end shows emphasis. This verb connects back to إِنَّآ (indeed we) at the start of the verse.
Basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something. This is a particle, so we start with its basic form rather than a root.
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for attaching the pronoun. This is just a spelling change.
Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people. This word is receiving the action of أَرْسَلْنَآ (we sent) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
The ًا ending shows this is being acted upon by the verb أَرْسَلْنَآ (we sent) in the verse
Basic meaning of seeing or witnessing something
Makes it a past tense verb meaning 'he witnessed'
Changed to active participle form meaning 'one who witnesses'
Added tanween to show this is describing the messenger (رَسُولًا). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this case.
This is a basic preposition showing something being above or having authority over something else
The ى changes to ي before adding a suffix - this is just a spelling change
Added كُمْ to show this is happening to multiple people being spoken to. This word shows who the sending (أَرْسَلْنَآ) was directed towards.
Basic comparison word used to show one thing is similar to another. Here it compares two instances of sending messengers.

steps.m: just as (comparing two instances of أَرْسَلْنَآ)
steps.n: Adding ما makes it mean 'just as' and connects two similar actions - sending a messenger to the readers and sending one to Pharaoh
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding hamza at the beginning makes the verb causative - showing someone made the sending happen
Adding نَا at the end changes the subject from 'he' to 'we'. The word is connected to فِرْعَوْنَ (Pharaoh) who was the recipient of the sending.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to فِرْعَوْنَ (Pharaoh) to show who the messenger was sent to.
The small alif (ىٰ) at the end is just a writing style that doesn't change the meaning. It shows this is the end of the word.

steps.m: Pharaoh (the title of ancient Egyptian kings)
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian language. It doesn't have an Arabic root.

steps.m: Pharaoh (as the object being sent to)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of sending (we sent to Pharaoh)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: messenger (someone who delivers messages as their role)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (to send) to a noun (messenger) by adding و

steps.m: a messenger (object of أَرْسَلْنَآ - we sent)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being sent in the sentence

steps.m: to disobey or rebel against authority

steps.n: Root letters got vowels (fathas) and the last letter ي became ى to show 'he' did the action in the past

steps.m: and/so he disobeyed (connected to أَخَذْنٰهُ - we seized him)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action is connected to what comes next in the verse (we seized him)
Added fatHa mark to show it's past tense verb

steps.m: Pharaoh (the title of ancient Egyptian kings who disobeyed عَصَىٰ)
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian language. In this ayah, it refers to the Pharaoh who disobeyed Allah's command.

steps.m: Pharaoh (as the one who disobeyed عَصَىٰ)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows Pharaoh is the one who did the disobeying mentioned in the previous word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
The word gets ال (al) at the start to mean 'the messenger' instead of just 'a messenger'
The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb 'disobeyed' in the phrase 'Pharaoh disobeyed the messenger'
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The نٰ shows 'we' did the action. This is Allah's response to Pharaoh's disobedience mentioned in the previous word فَعَصَىٰ (he disobeyed).
The هُ shows Pharaoh is being seized. This connects back to فِرْعَوْنُ (Pharaoh) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: and so we seized him (as punishment)
steps.n: The فَ shows this seizing was a direct result of the disobedience mentioned before. The next word أَخْذًا وَبِيلًا explains this was a severe/painful seizing.
Changed the root into a noun that describes the action of seizing
Added ًا to show this describes how we seized Pharaoh, connecting to the verb فَأَخَذْنٰهُ (then We seized him) in the ayah
Added ي between second and third root letters to create an adjective showing intensity

steps.m: severely/terribly (describing how Pharaoh was seized in فَأَخَذْنٰهُ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the seizing happened
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation: fatha on ك and sukun on ي

steps.m: so how? (connected to كَفَرْتُمْ - you disbelieved)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this question to the previous statement. It's asking 'so how will you guard against evil if you have disbelieved?'
Basic past tense form with weak letter ي at the end
The root letters were connected with vowels (َ) to make a past tense verb meaning 'he protected'. The ي changes to ى at the end.
Added تَ at start and doubled the middle letter to show doing the action to yourself
Added ون at the end to show many people are doing the action. The ى drops because it can't come before ون.
This is the basic form of the conditional particle that shows something depends on something else, like saying 'if you do this, then that will happen'
Added kasra mark under the إ to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added تُ at the end to change from 'he' to 'I' (first person) in past tense
Added مْ (m with sukun) to make it plural - now means 'you all' instead of 'you'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together. The و gets a sukun (ْ) because it's a weak letter in the middle.

steps.m: a day (object of تَتَّقُونَ - you guard against)
steps.n: The ًا (tanween fatḥa) shows this is the thing being acted upon by the verb تَتَّقُونَ (you guard against)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he makes/puts/sets (referring to the Day that makes children gray-haired)
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and added ُ at end to show it's doing the action. This verb describes what the terrifying Day will do to children.

steps.m: to be born, give birth, produce offspring
steps.n: The root becomes a noun referring to the result of birth - a child
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic noun for a single child
Changes to pattern فِعْلان to show multiple children
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific children, and added an alif mark (ٰ) and fatha at the end because it's being acted upon by the verb يَجْعَلُ (makes/turns)
Added kasra under ش to make it a noun meaning 'gray hair' instead of the action of turning gray

steps.m: gray-haired (describing what the children will be turned into)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon. In this ayah, it describes what يَجْعَلُ (makes/turns) will turn the children into.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding ا after second root letter turns verb into noun meaning 'sky'
Added ٱل to mean 'the' and ُ to show this is the subject doing the splitting in this verse
Connected the root letters with fathas to make basic past tense verb
Added اِنْ at beginning to show something happening by itself
Changed to start with مُنْ and middle vowel to ِ (kasra) to show something is currently splitting open

steps.m: being/becoming split open (describing السَّمَآءُ - the sky)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is describing the sky - like saying 'a split-open sky'
Added small meem after tanween to indicate nasalization before ب
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning by/with
Added هِ (it/him) to show what is being referred to. This connects to the word مُنفَطِرٌۢ (split apart) in the ayah, showing what is being split apart by.
Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation guide to show the vowel is elongated slightly
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the past tense. This word tells us about His promise (وَعْدُهُۥ) in this ayah.
Changes from root letters to noun meaning 'promise' by adding vowels
The ُ (damma) shows this word is doing the action of كَانَ (was) in this ayah
Adding هُ (his) at the end shows who made the promise. The word is still the subject of كَانَ (was).
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show what receives the action rather than what does the action
Added tanween alif to show this word is the object of كَانَ (was) in this ayah
This is an emphatic particle that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that something (تَذْكِرَةٌ) is definitely true.
The shadda (ّ) on the ن makes the emphasis even stronger, like saying 'most definitely' instead of just 'definitely'
This is the basic form of the word used to point to things
Added ذا to make it specifically point to something nearby
Changed ending to هِ to make it feminine, since it's pointing to a feminine word (تَذْكِرَةٌ meaning 'reminder')
Added small ي (ۦ) at the end as a pronunciation helper - it doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes verb 'to remind' into a noun meaning 'a reminder'

steps.m: a reminder (something that reminds) (subject of هٰذِهِۦ)
steps.n: Adding tanween shows this is indefinite. This word is what هٰذِهِۦ (this) refers to in the ayah.
This combines فَ (so/then) with مَن (who/whoever) to mean 'so whoever'
This is the root meaning showing the basic idea of wanting or wishing
Added fatha marks and connected the letters. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's between two fathas.
Added آ to show this is a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
This is the basic meaning of the root
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he took'

steps.m: he took/adopted for himself (subject of شَآءَ)
steps.n: Changed to Form VIII pattern by adding ت between first two root letters and putting kasra at start. This shows doing the action for oneself.

steps.m: he took/adopted for himself (subject of شَآءَ)
steps.n: Changed initial alif to waslah form since it's not needed for pronunciation when connected to previous word
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
The alif at the end is written with a small mark (ىٰ) to show it's a long vowel sound. This connects to the word رَبِّهِۦ (his Lord) to show movement or direction towards Him.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning the one who does this action completely
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something (comes before possessive pronoun)
Added هِ to show it belongs to him. The small ۦ helps pronunciation after the kasra. The word follows إِلَىٰ (to) in the ayah.

steps.m: to make a way or path
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of the root
Adding ي and vowels changes it from a verb into a noun meaning 'path'
Added tanween alif to show this is the object of the verb اتَّخَذَ (he took/adopted). In the ayah, it means 'he took a path to his Lord'

steps.m: indeed/truly (emphasizes that رَبَّكَ يَعْلَمُ is certain)
steps.n: This is a particle that adds emphasis to the statement that follows it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that 'your Lord knows' is absolutely certain.
Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb meaning 'he did X'

steps.m: your Lord (being acted upon by إِنَّ)
steps.n: The root رَبَبَ (nurture/raise) becomes رَبّ (Lord) with a doubled ب. Adding كَ at the end makes it 'your Lord'. The word gets a فتحة (a-vowel) at the end because of إِنَّ before it.
Basic meaning of knowing or having knowledge
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb

steps.m: he knows (that أَنَّكَ تَقُومُ - you stand up)
steps.n: Added يَ at start with sukun on middle letter and damma at end to show present tense. This verb tells what your Lord knows about your actions.
This is a particle that introduces a new clause and emphasizes what comes after it. It's connected to يَعْلَمُ (knows) in the previous part of the verse.
Added كَ at the end to show this refers to 'you'. This connects to يَعْلَمُ (knows) from before, so together it means 'knows that you...'
This root carries the basic meaning of standing or establishing something
Changed root letters into a basic present tense verb by adding vowels
Added تَ at beginning to show who is doing the action. This word is what your Lord (رَبَّكَ) knows (يَعْلَمُ) about - that you stand.
Basic vowels (fatha) added to make 'he was near' from root meaning 'near'
Added alif at start to make it mean 'causing nearness' instead of just 'being near'
The weak letter و changes to ى with alif above it when it comes at the end of this pattern
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or what it's made of. Here it connects to ثُلُثَىِ (two thirds) to show 'from two thirds'.
Adding the damma vowels creates a fraction noun meaning 'one third'
Added ى (alif maqsurah) at the end to change 'one third' into 'two thirds', like adding a special ending to show we mean exactly two
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word 'night' that comes after it
Connected the root letters to form the basic noun for night
Added the definite article 'the' and genitive case ending because this word follows مِن (from) in the ayah
The root letters ن-ص-ف became the noun 'half' by adding a kasra (ِ) under the ن and a sukun (ْ) on the ص. Like turning 'split' into 'half'.
Added هُ at the end to show it belongs to him (referring to the night mentioned earlier in the ayah)
Added وَ (wa) at the beginning to connect this to the previous parts about standing in prayer during different portions of the night
Adding the damma vowels changes it from a verb into a fraction noun meaning 'one third'
Added هُ to show possession ("his") and َ to show this is receiving the action of standing (تَقُومُ) in prayer
Added و with fatha to connect this to the previous parts of the night mentioned (نِصْفَهُۥ)

steps.m: to move in a circle or go around something

steps.n: Changed root to طَائِف (one who goes around) then added ة to make it mean 'a group' instead of one person
Added tanween to show it's indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English)
Added و at the beginning to connect it with what came before
Basic preposition that shows something comes from, is part of, or belongs to something else
Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word ٱلَّذِينَ (those who). This shows that 'from' is connecting to 'those who'.
This is a relative pronoun used to describe a group of people
This is a preposition meaning 'with' that connects to the verb تَقُومُ (you stand) in this ayah, showing who stands with the Prophet

steps.m: with you (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: The ك suffix shows this refers specifically to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, as he is being addressed in this part of the ayah about those who stand in prayer with him
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يُقَدِّرُ - determines)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show Allah is doing the action in this sentence - specifically the determining/measuring mentioned right after

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of يُقَدِّرُ - determines)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of يُقَدِّرُ - determines)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a waṣla form since it connects with the و before it in pronunciation

steps.m: to measure or determine with precision

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense or thorough
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding damma at end. This word shows what Allah does, as mentioned in وَٱللَّهُ (and Allah) earlier in the ayah.
Connected the root letters to form the basic noun for night
Added ال to make it specific. The ل gets a shadda because two ل letters combine.
Added fatha at the end because 'night' is the object of the verb يُقَدِّرُ (determines/measures) in this ayah

steps.m: to flow brightly or shine forth

steps.n: Forms noun meaning daytime from root concept of light flowing forth
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which makes the next letter double-strength (with shadda)
Added 'and' at beginning and case ending to show this is the object of Allah's measuring/determining in the ayah

steps.m: to know or understand something deeply

steps.n: Added vowels to show this happened in the past. This word connects to سَيَكُونُ (will be) in the ayah, showing what Allah knew would happen.

steps.m: that (introduces the clause سَيَكُونُ 'there will be')
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a new clause. In this ayah, it connects عَلِمَ (He knew) with what He knew: that there would be sick people among you.
Starting with the two separate parts: لَ (not) and أَنْ (that)
The أ is dropped when the two parts combine, changing the meaning to specifically refer to future events

steps.m: will never (negates تُحْصُوهُ - you will count it)
steps.n: The ل gets doubled (shadda) to connect smoothly with the next word. It still means 'will never' and connects to تُحْصُوهُ (you will count it) to say you won't be able to count it.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense (he) to present tense (you/she)

steps.m: you all cannot keep track of it
steps.n: Changed the ending to وه to mean 'you all' (plural) and added هُ meaning 'it' at the end. Works with لَّن earlier to mean 'cannot'.

steps.m: to return or turn back to what is right
he turned back (to what is right)
steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowel marks to show this happened in the past. The و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.

steps.m: so He turned (in mercy) (connected to عَلِمَ - He knew)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action happened as a result of what came before. In this ayah, Allah is the subject who turns in mercy to the believers after knowing they cannot count the exact hours.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he read' to 'read!' by adding U-sound at start and making middle quiet - this makes it a command
Added وا at the end to make it a command for multiple people. The hamza (ء) replaces أ when adding وا.
Added فَ (fa) at start to show this command comes as a result of what was mentioned before. The alif gets a small circle (ٱ) because it comes after فَ.
This is a particle that introduces a relative clause, connecting to تَيَسَّرَ (became easy). Together they mean 'whatever became easy'.
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter creates Form II, changing meaning from 'to be easy' to 'to make easy'

steps.m: became easy (what to read from القُرْءَانِ)
steps.n: Adding تَ at the beginning shows the action happens by itself. This word describes what to read from the Quran - whatever becomes easy.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. In this ayah, it connects 'what is easy' to 'the Quran', showing what to read from.
Added fatha at the end because it connects to the next word (القُرْءَانِ). This helps make the pronunciation flow smoothly between words.
Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'that which is recited frequently' - this special pattern is mainly used for the Quran itself
Adding 'ال' makes it specific - not just any recitation, but THE recitation (the Quran)
Added kasra at the end because this word follows مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Connected the root letters together to form the base word

steps.m: he knew (Allah knew that you would not be able to calculate it)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show past tense. In this ayah, Allah is the subject who knew about the inability of people to calculate the night portions precisely.
Basic connecting word that introduces what Allah knew. In this ayah, it connects عَلِمَ (He knew) with what follows.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing vowels to show ongoing action
Added سَ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or is part of. In this ayah, it's connected to the verb سَيَكُونُ (will be) to show who will be in certain states.

steps.m: from/among you all (subjects of سَيَكُونُ - will be)
steps.n: The كُم at the end means 'you all' (when talking to a group). This shows that some from among the group being addressed will be in the states mentioned after this word.
This is the basic concept of sickness or illness
Changes from verb 'to be sick' into the noun 'sickness' by adding specific vowel marks
Adding ى makes this refer to people who are sick, and makes it plural

steps.m: to come after or be different from something
steps.n: This root is about something coming after or being delayed

steps.m: others (who are different/separate from the first group)
steps.n: The root أخر becomes آخَر (other/another) and ون is added to make it plural - meaning 'others' or 'different people'

steps.m: and others (who are different/separate from the first group)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this to the previous group mentioned
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The ي prefix changes this to a present tense verb for 'he'

steps.m: they move through/travel (seeking فَضْلِ اللَّهِ - Allah's bounty)
steps.n: The ونَ suffix changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural). In this verse, it refers to those traveling to seek Allah's bounty.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it shows the location where people are traveling - in/through the earth.
This root refers to the earth or ground we walk on
Adding vowel marks to make the word pronounceable
Added the definite article ال to specify we're talking about THE earth
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added اِ at beginning and ت after first root letter to show doing the action for oneself with extra effort
Changed from past to present tense by replacing اِ with يَ at the beginning - both forms mean 'to seek earnestly for oneself'
Added ون ending to show multiple people doing the action

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group something belongs to. In this ayah, it connects 'sick ones' (مَّرْضَىٰ) with 'you all' (كُم) to mean 'sick ones from among you'.

steps.m: to give extra, to favor, to be generous

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'favor' or 'bounty'. This is something being given by Allah.
Added kasra at the end to show this favor belongs to or comes from Allah. The phrase فَضْلِ ٱللَّهِ means 'Allah's favor' or 'the favor from Allah'.
Changed إِلَه to اللَه to make it a proper name for The One True God. The ال here is part of the name itself, not just meaning 'the'.
Added ِ at the end to show possession, and added shadda on ل. This word is possessed by فَضْلِ (bounty) in the phrase 'bounty of Allah'.
Added alif and vowel marks to create the singular form meaning 'other'
Added ون to make it plural, meaning multiple others

steps.m: and others (who are seeking Allah's bounty)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the verse. These 'others' refer to people traveling for trade or business, as explained in the rest of the verse (يَضْرِبُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ يَبْتَغُونَ مِن فَضْلِ ٱللَّهِ)
Adding alif shows the action is mutual - both sides are fighting each other
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to ـِ (kasra). Both words mean fighting with someone else (mutual action).
The suffix ون changes the verb from singular (he fights) to plural (they fight)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it connects 'traveling' (يَضْرِبُونَ) with 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ) to mean 'traveling in/throughout the earth'
Added ي between second and third letters to make the word into its standard noun form
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'path of Allah'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of فَضْلِ (bounty). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to read or recite something carefully

steps.n: Adding alif at start turns it into an instruction to read
Added فَ to show this command comes as a result of what was mentioned before - that Allah has accepted their inability to pray all night
This is a relative pronoun that means 'whatever' or 'what' when it connects to another word. Here it connects to تَيَسَّرَ (is easy) to mean 'whatever is easy'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Intensifies meaning from 'being easy' to 'making something easy'

steps.m: it became easy (from مِنَ ٱلْقُرْءَانِ - from the Quran)
steps.n: The تَ prefix shows the action happening by itself. In this ayah, it refers to whatever portion of the Quran becomes easy to recite.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or moves away from
The هُ at the end means 'it' or 'him' - it's attached to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to ٱلْقُرْءَانِ (the Quran), meaning 'whatever is easy from it (the Quran)'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ي, and the first letter gets an أ to mean 'I' instead of 'he'
Adding وا changes it from 'I establish' to 'you all establish'
Added و at beginning to connect this command with previous statements
Added basic vowel marks to connect the root letters
The root word gets a special mark (ة) at the end to make it mean 'prayer' as a noun instead of the action 'to pray'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ص because it comes after ال

steps.m: the prayer (object of وَأَقِيمُوا - and establish)
steps.n: Shows this is what's being established in the command 'establish prayer'
The basic meaning is about coming with something or giving
Changed from basic root form to a command meaning 'you give!' by adding ءَا at start and ُ at end
Adding وا makes it a command for multiple people
وَ means 'and' - it connects this command to the previous one

steps.m: to purify or make something grow through giving
steps.n: The root meaning is about making something pure and helping it grow
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the word into a noun meaning the act or result of purifying. The ة at the end makes it a specific instance or type of purification.
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning and َ at the end to show this is the object being given. The ز gets a shadda because it combines with the ل from ٱل.

steps.m: to cut off a portion, to lend
steps.n: The original meaning was about cutting something off - came to mean lending because you 'cut off' part of your wealth
Added vowel marks to make 'he cut off/lent' in past tense
Changed to a command form by adding أ at start and changing vowels - tells one person to lend
Added و at beginning to connect this command with previous commands in the verse (أَقِيمُوا۟ and ءَاتُوا۟). This is telling believers to lend to Allah.
This is the three-letter root that gives us words about divinity and worship
Root meaning 'worship' became a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'
Changed from 'a deity' to 'The One True God' by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being lent to in أَقْرِضُوا۟)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because Allah is receiving the action of lending in this verse. The hamza at the start is written differently but pronounced the same.
Same root as the previous word, about cutting/lending
Transforms from verbal concept to noun meaning 'loan' using common verbal noun pattern

steps.m: a loan (object of أَقْرِضُوا - give as loan)
steps.n: The ًا shows this is the thing being loaned (object of 'give as loan')
This root relates to goodness and beauty in both appearance and character
Adding vowel marks changes this from a root into an actual adjective meaning 'good'

steps.m: good (as the object being lent in قَرْضًا حَسَنًا - a good loan)
steps.n: Added an -an ending to show this word describes the loan (قَرْضًا) mentioned in the verse
This is a basic particle that can mean 'whatever' or 'what' depending on context. In this ayah, it refers to whatever good deeds you send ahead.

steps.m: and whatever (good deeds you send ahead)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. This connects the concept of good deeds with the previous discussion about prayer and charity.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intensive or causative
تُ shows it's either 'you' (male) or 'she' doing the action
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action
The meaning changed to 'self' because it's the precious, breathing part of a person
Basic root letters got vowels to make the word mean 'one soul/self'
Added hamza at start and damma on last letter to make it plural (for 3-10 things)
Added kasra at the end because it follows لِ (to/for)
Added كُم at the end to show it belongs to multiple people being addressed
Added لِ at beginning to show this is for/to something
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added shadda for pronunciation because it comes after a connecting word. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
The middle letter 'ya' is a weak letter that affects the word's formation
Root letters got vowels (fatha-sukun) to make the noun meaning 'good/better/best'

steps.m: any good thing (that you send ahead)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is referring to any good thing. In this ayah, it refers to any good deeds or charity you send ahead for yourself.
Basic past tense form showing someone did the action
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from singular 'you find' to plural 'you all find' by adding و
Added هُ at the end to show what is being found
This root carries the basic idea of being present or being near to something
Adding the kasra (ِ) under the first letter changes this from a concept into a word showing location or presence
Adding the fatha (َ) at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it (اللَّهِ - Allah)
Changed إِلَه to اللَه to make it a proper name for The One True God. The ال here is part of the name itself, not just meaning 'the'.
Added ِ at the end to show this connects to the word فَضْلِ (bounty), meaning 'bounty of Allah'. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
Basic pronoun that refers back to the good deeds mentioned in the previous part of the verse. It's the subject of what you'll find with Allah.
Basic noun pattern showing the quality of being good

steps.m: better/best (object of تَجِدُوهُ - you will find it)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon by the verb تَجِدُوهُ (you will find it) in this ayah
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make something great' instead of 'be great'
Adding conjunction وَ meaning 'and'

steps.m: reward, payment (acted on by أَعْظَمَ - greater)
steps.n: Connected the root letters together to make a noun meaning the result of paying/rewarding. This word is being acted on by أَعْظَمَ (greater) in this ayah.

steps.m: a reward (as something being acted on)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is an indefinite noun being acted upon
Added ٱسْتَ at the beginning to show seeking or requesting forgiveness from someone. Like saying 'ask for forgiveness' instead of just 'forgive'
Added 'wa' (وَ) at start meaning 'and', plus damma (ُ) at end to show 'I' am doing the action
Changed the word into a command for multiple people by adding وا at the end. Like changing 'seek' to 'all of you seek!'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object of أَقْرِضُوا - lend to)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a mark at the end (fatha) to show it's receiving the action of lending
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain. Here it emphasizes that 'your Lord knows' is definitely true.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special divine name

steps.m: Allah (as the object being asked for forgiveness from)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being asked for forgiveness from in this ayah
Added و and vowels to show this describes someone who forgives again and again
The ٌ (tanween) at the end shows this is a complete word/name describing Allah, not connected to other words
The ي makes it mean 'very merciful' instead of just 'merciful'

steps.m: one who shows very intense mercy (as a quality)
steps.n: Added two dots (tanween) to show this is a complete description of Allah's endless mercy
